description: Construct a triangle from two sides and the included angle between them (Side-Angle-Side). Vertex A is at the base point (or origin if omitted). Side b goes from A to C, side c goes from A to B, and angleA is the angle at vertex A.
utility: Create triangle from two sides and the included angle between them

arguments.name: b
arguments.description: Length of side from A to C (opposite vertex B). Must be positive.

arguments.name: angleA
arguments.description: Angle at vertex A in degrees (must be between 0 and 180, exclusive)

arguments.name: c
arguments.description: Length of side from A to B (opposite vertex C). Must be positive.

arguments.name: basePoint
arguments.description: Position for vertex A (defaults to origin). Must be a point variable or point(G, x, y).

arguments.name: angle
arguments.description: Rotation angle in degrees for the entire triangle around vertex A

alternates.description: Triangle at origin with two sides and included angle
Isosceles triangle with equal sides and 60-degree angle

examples.scenario: SAS triangle for sine law proof with sides 5 and 6 at 40°
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(-10, 11.4), size(25), range(-1, 8), grid(noaxes), type(geometry)); triangle_1 = sas(graph_1, 5, 40, 6, point(graph_1, 0, 0))

examples.scenario: Extract edges as lines for labeling and measurement
examples.expression: line_c = line(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(edge), 1), type(segment), c(blue), defer()); line_a = line(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(edge), 2), type(segment), c(blue), defer()); line_b = line(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(edge), 3), type(segment), c(blue), defer())

examples.scenario: Extract and label all three angles in a sine law proof
examples.expression: angle_A = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 1)); label_A = label(graph_1, at(angle_A), "A", buff(-1.0, 1.0)); angle_B = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 2)); label_B = label(graph_1, at(angle_B), "B", buff(-1, -2.3))

examples.scenario: Animated SAS triangle with variable angle
examples.expression: mathexpr_1 = variableanimator(at(1,4), "t", 20, 120); graph_1 = g2d(at(2,18), size(25.9), range(-6, 7), type(geometry)); triangle_1 = sas(graph_1, 5, t, 6, point(graph_1, 0, 0)); playanim_1 = playanimator(mathexpr_1, t(3))
measure_a = measure(graph_1, line_a, buff(0.7, -1.2), c(black)); measure_b = measure(graph_1, line_b, buff(0.6, -0.4))
